Mez..personality: A restless, nervous individual who is compelled to constantly move and change positions. They are indifferent to everybody and everything, yet cannot bear to be alone, while simultaneously being averse to talking. They tend to externalize their suffering — when skin symptoms are present they are in fairly good health, but when these are suppressed they become a mental and physical wreck.
Mez..mental_state: Thought is difficult, memory is weak, and the mind is absent and unfocused. When eruptions are suppressed, strange mental symptoms emerge including melancholy that fixates on religious or financial matters, shaping itself around the patient's business concerns. In advanced states, the patient deteriorates into insanity with deep sadness and cognitive decline.
Mez..emotional_pattern: Dominated by a deep-seated apprehensiveness felt physically in the pit of the stomach — every shock, bad news, or unexpected event triggers a faint, gone, frightened sensation in the solar plexus. They are irritable yet melancholic, swinging between agitated restlessness and profound indifference. There is a pervasive undercurrent of anxiety and vulnerability, as if something dreadful is always about to happen.
